tools.name: display_render
tools.description:
Render drawing commands on the AI Canvas e-ink display. Display is 800x480 pixels, black and white only. Coordinates: (0,0) is top-left, x horizontal (0-799), y vertical (0-479). Commands execute in order; display refreshes after all complete (~2-3s). Returns 429 if busy; retry after 3s.

RENDERING MODEL:
Commands render sequentially — later commands overwrite earlier ones (no transparency). Each pixel is black or white. Without a 'clear' command, new commands draw on top of existing content. Include {op:'clear'} first to start fresh.

CHARACTER SET:
ASCII 0x20-0x7E only (space through ~). Do NOT use unicode, degree symbols (use 'F' not '°F'), em dashes (use '--'), curly quotes, or emoji.

COLORS: 'black' or 'white'. Default: black for drawing, white for clear.

OPERATIONS:
- clear: {color} - default white
- pixel: {x, y, color}
- line: {x0, y0, x1, y1, color, thickness(default 1)} - also accepts x/y as aliases for x0/y0
- rect: {x, y, w, h, color, fill, radius, stroke, strokeWidth} - fill defaults TRUE (solid). Set fill:false for outline. radius (rounded corners) ONLY works with fill:true. stroke/strokeWidth add a border on top.
- circle: {x(center), y(center), r, color, fill(default true)}
- ellipse: {x(center), y(center), rx, ry, color, fill(default true)}
- arc: {x(center), y(center), r, startAngle, endAngle, color} - angles: 0=east, clockwise. 1px outline only.
- polygon: {points:[{x,y},...], color, fill(default true)} - min 3, max 64 points. Outline always drawn.
- polyline: {points:[{x,y},...], color} - min 2, max 64 points. No thickness.
- text: {x, y, text, font, size, bold, color, align, valign, maxWidth, maxHeight, overflow, lineHeight} - see TEXT LAYOUT below
- image: {x, y, w, h, data(base64 PNG), dither} - dither: 'threshold'|'ordered'(default)|'floyd_steinberg'. w/h = target display size.
- gradient: {x, y, w, h, direction('horizontal'|'vertical'), from, to} - dithered. Defaults: horizontal, black->white.
- flood_fill: {x, y, color} - WARNING: queue limit 4096 pixels, large fills incomplete. Use rect for large solid areas.
- clip: {x, y, w, h} - one clip at a time, new replaces old
- unclip: {} - remove clip
- raw_bitmap: {x, y, w, h, data(base64)} - 1-bit packed, MSB=leftmost, rows byte-aligned, 1=white 0=black
- qr: {x, y, size, data, color, ec} - QR code. size=square px (modules scale to fit). data=string to encode. ec: 'L'|'M'(default)|'Q'|'H'. Includes 2-module quiet zone.

METADATA:
Any command can include 'meta' (freeform object). Stored and returned by GET /canvas for multi-agent coordination. Example: {"op":"text","x":30,"y":72,"text":"72F","meta":{"type":"weather"}}. Frame-level meta: {"commands":[...],"meta":{"agent":"my-agent"}}.

TEXT MEASUREMENT: Use POST /measure (or text_measure tool) to get pixel-perfect text dimensions before rendering.

=== TEXT LAYOUT (CRITICAL - read before placing text) ===

Defaults when omitted: font='sans', size=12, bold=false, align='left', lineHeight=1.2.

The y parameter is the TOP of the text block, but the first baseline renders at y + yAdvance. Visible glyph pixels start at approximately y + 0.6 * yAdvance.

Font metrics:
  sans  9pt: yAdvance=22, line height=26px
  sans 12pt: yAdvance=29, line height=34px
  sans 18pt: yAdvance=42, line height=50px
  sans 24pt: yAdvance=56, line height=67px
  mono  9pt: yAdvance=18, line height=21px

Text supports \n for manual line breaks.

SPACING RULES (prevent overlaps):
1. Total height of N lines = N * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight). Next element goes at y + this value or below.
2. Text inside filled rect: rect h >= yAdvance, set text y = rect_y (bottom-aligned). For centering: text y = rect_y - (yAdvance - rect_h)/3. Negative y is safe (clipped). Example: 60px bar at y=0 with size 24: set text y=-10.
3. Align shapes (circles, rects as bullets) with text: shape_center_y = text_y + 0.8 * yAdvance. Example: text y=100 size 12 -- bullet circle cy=123.
4. Same baseline for mixed sizes: y_a + yAdvance_a == y_b + yAdvance_b.
5. Estimate wrapped lines: chars_per_line ~ maxWidth / (yAdvance * 0.45).

Alignment: Without maxWidth, x is anchor (left/center/right per align). With maxWidth, x is LEFT edge of text box, text aligns within.

valign: REQUIRES maxHeight to work. Without maxHeight, valign has no effect.

overflow: 'clip'(default) hides overflow. 'truncate' adds '...' to last line. 'shrink' tries smaller fonts 24->18->12->9 (bold is lost).

COMMON MISTAKES (read this before rendering):
1. ALWAYS compute text block height before placing the next element: block_h = num_lines * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight). Next element y >= prev_y + block_h.
2. ALWAYS estimate wrapped line count when using maxWidth: num_lines = ceil(char_count / floor(maxWidth / (yAdvance * 0.45))). Account for manual \n adding extra lines.
3. NEVER set maxHeight smaller than the text needs — it silently clips lines. Required: maxHeight >= num_lines * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight).
4. NEVER eyeball y positions — calculate every y from the previous element's y + block_h.
5. Keep status bar text short — long strings get clipped at display edge (800px).

BATCHED RENDERING:\nFor complex scenes (many commands or polyline/polygon points), split into multiple smaller requests with refresh:'none', then send a final request with refresh:'full'. Each batch draws to the framebuffer without refreshing the display. The final batch triggers one screen refresh. Always include 'clear' only in the FIRST batch.\n\nLIMITS: JSON payload max ~32KB (effective ~10KB for point-heavy payloads due to JSON parser memory). Polygon/polyline max 64 points. Flood fill max 4096px queue.

VALIDATION MODE:
Pass "validate":true in the request body (or ?validate query param) to get per-command bounding boxes and layout verification in the response. The response will include a "validation" object with:
- commands: array of {bbox:[x,y,w,h], off_screen, lines, truncated} for each command
- text_overlaps: pairs of command indices where two text bounding boxes overlap (almost always a layout bug)
- off_screen: list of command indices with content extending beyond the 800x480 display
Use validation to verify renders without downloading screenshots. Check text_overlaps to catch spacing errors, off_screen to find clipped content, and bbox positions to verify layout.

DRY-RUN MODE (RECOMMENDED WORKFLOW):
Pass "dryRun":true to validate layout WITHOUT touching the display. No pixels are drawn, no framebuffer is modified, no display refresh happens. The response includes the same validation data as validate:true. Use this workflow:
1. Send commands with dryRun:true
2. Check text_overlaps and off_screen in the response
3. If issues found, adjust coordinates and repeat step 1
4. When validation passes (both arrays empty), send the same commands WITHOUT dryRun to render
This avoids multiple slow e-ink refreshes (~3s each) while iterating on layout.
Circuit breaker: after 3 consecutive dry-runs, the 4th dryRun request auto-promotes to a real render (status:'ok', dry_run_overridden:true). The response includes dry_runs_remaining to track this. Any real render resets the counter.

Example - dashboard with header bar, content, status bar:
[
  {"op":"clear","color":"white"},
  {"op":"rect","x":0,"y":0,"w":800,"h":42,"color":"black","fill":true},
  {"op":"text","x":20,"y":6,"text":"DASHBOARD","font":"sans","size":12,"bold":true,"color":"white"},
  {"op":"text","x":30,"y":50,"text":"Section Title","font":"sans","size":9,"bold":true},
  {"op":"text","x":30,"y":72,"text":"Large Text","font":"sans","size":24,"bold":true},
  {"op":"text","x":30,"y":130,"text":"Detail line","font":"sans","size":9},
  {"op":"line","x0":20,"y0":158,"x1":780,"y1":158},
  {"op":"text","x":30,"y":164,"text":"SECTION 2","font":"sans","size":9,"bold":true},
  {"op":"text","x":30,"y":186,"text":"Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3","font":"sans","size":12,"maxWidth":740},
  {"op":"rect","x":0,"y":458,"w":800,"h":22,"color":"black","fill":true},
  {"op":"text","x":10,"y":462,"text":"status bar text","font":"mono","size":9,"bold":true,"color":"white","align":"center","maxWidth":780}
]

commands.description: Array of drawing commands to execute in order

y.description: Y coordinate (vertical, 0=top, max 479). For text: top of text block; first baseline at y + yAdvance

radius.description: Corner radius for rounded rect, or alias for r

fill.description: Fill shape (default: TRUE — shapes are solid). Set false for outline. Rect rounded corners (radius) only work when fill is true.

text.description: Text content. ASCII 0x20-0x7E only. Supports \n for line breaks.

valign.description: Vertical text alignment. REQUIRES maxHeight to be set — no effect without it.

maxWidth.description: Max text width for word wrap (pixels). With maxWidth, x is always the LEFT edge of the text box.

maxHeight.description: Max text height (pixels). Lines exceeding this are clipped/truncated/shrunk per overflow.

overflow.description: Text overflow when maxHeight exceeded. 'clip'(default): hide. 'truncate': adds '...'. 'shrink': tries smaller fonts (loses bold).

points.description: Array of {x,y} points. Polygon: min 3, max 64. Polyline: min 2, max 64.

startAngle.description: Arc start angle in degrees. 0=east, increases clockwise.

endAngle.description: Arc end angle in degrees. 0=east, increases clockwise.

ec.description: QR error correction: L=7%, M=15%(default), Q=25%, H=30%

meta.description: Optional freeform metadata for multi-agent coordination. Stored and returned by GET /canvas.

zone.description: Optional zone ID for partial update

refresh.description: Display refresh mode. 'none': draw to framebuffer without refreshing the display. Use for batched rendering: send multiple small requests with refresh:'none', then a final request with refresh:'full' to flush to screen. This bypasses the single-request JSON memory limit.

validate.description: Enable render validation. Response will include per-command bounding boxes, text-text overlap detection, and off-screen content warnings. Use this instead of downloading screenshots to verify layout correctness.

dryRun.description: Dry-run mode: validate layout WITHOUT drawing to the display. Returns the same validation data as validate:true (bounding boxes, text overlaps, off-screen warnings) but does NOT render pixels, commit to the framebuffer, or trigger a display refresh. Use this to iterate on layout until text_overlaps and off_screen are empty, then send the final version without dryRun to actually render. Status will be 'dry_run' instead of 'ok'. Implies validate:true. Circuit breaker: after 3 consecutive dry-runs the device auto-renders the 4th request (dry_run_overridden:true in response). Response includes dry_runs_remaining.

tools.name: display_info
tools.description: Get AI Canvas display capabilities, dimensions (800x480, B/W), available fonts, and device status.

tools.name: text_measure
tools.description: Measure text dimensions without rendering. Returns pixel-perfect width, height, line count, and font metrics. Use this to plan layouts precisely instead of estimating. Supports batch mode (array of requests) to measure multiple strings in one call.

measurements.description: Array of text measurement requests. Each returns {width, height, lines, truncated, yAdvance, lineHeight}.

maxWidth.description: Max width for word wrap (px). 0=no wrap.
